description: Iraqi forces invade Kuwait; fears of prolonged supply disruption from two major OPEC producers trigger an immediate price spike and the run-up to the Gulf War.

event_name: Gulf War - Operation Desert Storm begins
description: US-led coalition launches military action to expel Iraq from Kuwait. Uncertainty resolves quickly once coalition air superiority is established and prices fall sharply from their pre-war spike.
Collapse of the Thai baht triggers a regional financial crisis across East and Southeast Asia, sharply reducing energy demand forecasts for the region.
Failure of OPEC to agree effective production cuts amid Asian demand weakness pushes Brent to historic lows near $10/barrel by December 1998.
OPEC and several non-OPEC producers agree substantial coordinated output cuts, beginning the price recovery from the 1998 lows.
Attacks on the US raise fears of a global economic slowdown and a sharp contraction in travel and fuel demand, pressuring prices downward.
The Iraq War begins, raising fears over Iraqi and regional supply security and adding a sustained geopolitical risk premium to prices.

event_name: Brent oil price reaches pre-crisis record high (~$147.5/bbl)
description: Strong global demand, a weak US dollar, and speculative activity drive Brent to its all-time nominal high just before the financial crisis hits.
The collapse of Lehman Brothers triggers a global financial crisis and demand collapse, causing Brent prices to crash from record highs to below $40/barrel within months.
Widespread political unrest across the Arab world, including civil war in Libya, disrupts North African oil exports and raises supply-risk premiums.

event_name: OPEC declines to cut production amid oversupply
description: Facing a global supply glut driven partly by rising US shale output, OPEC (led by Saudi Arabia) opts to defend market share rather than cut output, triggering the 2014-2016 price collapse.

event_name: Oil price trough (~$27/bbl) and Iran sanctions relief
description: Brent hits a 13-year low as the oversupply glut persists and Iran ramps up exports following the lifting of nuclear-related sanctions in January 2016.

event_name: OPEC+ first coordinated production cut agreement
description: OPEC agrees its first output cut since 2008 and, unusually, brings non-OPEC producers led by Russia into a coordinated "OPEC+" cut, supporting a price recovery.

event_name: US withdraws from Iran nuclear deal
description: The US exits the JCPOA and reimposes sanctions on Iranian oil exports, tightening expected global supply.
OPEC+ talks collapse when Russia refuses further cuts; Saudi Arabia responds by ramping up production and slashing prices, crashing the market just as COVID-19 demand losses begin.

event_name: OPEC+ record production cut amid COVID-19 demand collapse
description: Facing an unprecedented demand collapse from COVID-19 lockdowns, OPEC+ agrees a record ~9.7 million bpd production cut; days later US WTI futures briefly turn negative.
Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine raises fears of severe supply disruption from a top-3 global oil producer and triggers sweeping Western sanctions discussions, pushing Brent above $130/bbl.
